name: Bob & Helen Parr
fixtureLineage: UI-shape lift of mrT-family.json v1.1 (archetype 16, uk-family-accumulation). Exercises HICBC taper, JISA, education funding, life-cover-in-trust, family protection gap, no-will/no-guardian risk.

lifeInsurance.note: A second £200k single-life policy (2022) claims to be in trust but the trust deed was never provided — treated as inside the estate until verified.

alerts.headline: No will and no guardian named — with a 13-year-old at home
alerts.context: If you both died tonight, the law (not you) decides who raises Dash and how £427k is split. Intestacy gives cohabiting relatives nothing automatically.
alerts.action: Start a will + name a guardian

alerts.headline: Child benefit charge is clawing back £1,739 a year
alerts.context: Bob's income (£68.5k) sits in the £60k–£80k claw-back band: 78% of your child benefit is taken back. Pension contributions lower the income HMRC counts.

alerts.headline: £200k of life cover may sit inside your estate
alerts.context: The 2022 policy claims to be in trust but no trust deed was ever provided. Until verified, it pays into the estate — slower, and potentially taxed.

alerts.headline: University costs £46.5k — the fund holds £18k
alerts.context: Violet has 3 years of university remaining at ~£15.5k/yr. The earmarked education fund covers under half; the gap is £28.5k.

alerts.headline: £4,800 of Dash's JISA allowance unused
alerts.context: The Junior ISA has £4,200 in of a £9,000 yearly allowance. It resets on 5 April and unused allowance is lost.

timeline.title: Dash starts university (projected)
timeline.sub: Projected ~£19k/yr · JISA is the funding vehicle

aiSuggestions: Why am I losing most of my child benefit and how do I stop it?
What happens to the kids and the house if we both die without a will?
How do we close the £28.5k university funding gap for Violet?
Should Helen pay more into her pension while working part-time?
Is our life insurance actually in trust — and why does that matter?

behaviour.risk: Good saving habits — regular pension and ISA contributions, a dedicated education pot, and a JISA running for Dash.
behaviour.action: Set a yearly money date: JISA top-up before 5 April, nominations check, and a premium review.
behaviour.future: Steady contributions through your 40s do more for this family than any clever product ever will.

capital.risk: Net worth around £427k, but £485k of gross value sits in the home — your accessible investments are a small slice of the total.
capital.action: Keep building the ISA and pensions so wealth grows outside the front door as well as behind it.
capital.future: At current contribution rates the invested pot roughly doubles by the time Dash finishes university.

tax.risk: Bob's £68.5k income sits in the child-benefit claw-back band — £1,739 a year is being taken back, an effective tax spike most families never spot.
tax.action: Extra pension contributions reduce the income HMRC counts; enough contribution takes you below £60k and keeps the full child benefit.
tax.future: Helen's unused basic-rate band and allowances are the family's cheapest tax lever as her hours grow.

protection.risk: £350k joint cover is properly in trust — but the £200k 2022 policy is unverified, there's no income protection, and no will or guardian for Dash.
protection.action: Three fixes in order: write wills + name a guardian, verify the 2022 trust paperwork, price income protection for Bob.
protection.future: A family with one main earner and a 13-year-old depends more on paperwork than on portfolio returns.

cashflow.risk: Roughly £700/mo surplus after essentials, pension saving and the loan — healthy, but university costs are about to bite for 3 years.
cashflow.action: Direct surplus to the education gap first; it has a hard deadline the pension doesn't.
cashflow.future: When the personal loan clears (~2 years) another £165/mo frees up automatically.

debt.risk: £278k mortgage at 3.9% fixed to 2029 plus a small £4.2k personal loan at 6.8% — manageable at 25% of gross income.
debt.action: Clear the 6.8% loan ahead of schedule if surplus allows; diary the remortgage for early 2029.
debt.future: On the current path the mortgage clears around Bob's 61st birthday — before retirement, which is the test that matters.

estate.risk: No will means intestacy rules decide everything — and with no guardian named, a court chooses who raises Dash.
estate.action: Wills and guardianship first; they cost less than a family holiday and remove the single biggest risk on this page.
estate.future: Once wills exist, the £200k trust verification and pension nominations finish the job.

plans.label: Wills + guardian for Dash + LPA
plans.target_display: Wills written · guardian named · LPAs in place

plans.label: Close the £28.5k university gap for Violet
Pension contributions to escape the child-benefit claw-back

scenarios.name: Bob salary-sacrifices £9k to escape the claw-back
